response.justification: The candidate is a generalist web and full-stack developer whose career is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and JavaScript. C++ experience is narrow and dated, limited to a single role writing components for NI LabWindows/CVI and a bachelor thesis project—neither of which aligns with Windows-platform capital-markets systems. There is no evidence of fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge applied with C++; the one finance-adjacent role (investment platform in Germany) used a TypeScript/GraphQL stack. SQL Server or Oracle expertise is absent, with database exposure limited to MongoDB and MySQL.
strengths: Has baseline C++ exposure from an NI LabWindows engineering tools role and academic sound-synthesis project
Finance-adjacent engagement (investment platform, Germany) demonstrates some familiarity with regulated, business-critical software delivery
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and conference-level knowledge-sharing show professional maturity
concerns: C++ is a peripheral skill across a 15+ year career overwhelmingly built on web technologies; no evidence of 5–10 years of production C++ on Windows
Zero domain expertise in fixed income, fixed income derivatives, or capital markets systems—the mandatory domain requirement is unmet
No demonstrated depth in SQL Server or Oracle; database work centers on MongoDB and MySQL, which are misaligned with enterprise capital-markets backends
